state.output:
<skill_content name="gws-gmail">
# Skill: gws-gmail

# gmail (v1)

> **PREREQUISITE:** Read `../gws-shared/SKILL.md` for auth, global flags, and security rules. If missing, run `gws generate-skills` to create it.

```bash
gws gmail <resource> <method> [flags]
```

## Helper Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| [`+send`](../gws-gmail-send/SKILL.md) | Send an email |
| [`+triage`](../gws-gmail-triage/SKILL.md) | Show unread inbox summary (sender, subject, date) |
| [`+watch`](../gws-gmail-watch/SKILL.md) | Watch for new emails and stream them as NDJSON |

## API Resources

### users

  - `getProfile` — Gets the current user's Gmail profile.
  - `stop` — Stop receiving push notifications for the given user mailbox.
  - `watch` — Set up or update a push notification watch on the given user mailbox.
  - `drafts` — Operations on the 'drafts' resource
  - `history` — Operations on the 'history' resource
  - `labels` — Operations on the 'labels' resource
  - `messages` — Operations on the 'messages' resource
  - `settings` — Operations on the 'settings' resource
  - `threads` — Operations on the 'threads' resource

## Discovering Commands

Before calling any API method, inspect it:

```bash
# Browse resources and methods
gws gmail --help

# Inspect a method's required params, types, and defaults
gws schema gmail.<resource>.<method>
```

Use `gws schema` output to build your `--params` and `--json` flags.

Base directory for this skill: file:///F:/Aniruddha/code/college/mcpserver/.opencode/skills/gws-gmail
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

<skill_files>

</skill_files>
</skill_content>
state.title: Loaded skill: gws-gmail

input.command: gws gmail --help 2>&1 | head -40

state.output:
The Gmail API lets you view and manage Gmail mailbox data like threads, messages, and labels.

Usage: gws [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  +send       [Helper] Send an email
  +triage     [Helper] Show unread inbox summary (sender, subject, date)
  +reply      [Helper] Reply to a message (handles threading automatically)
  +reply-all  [Helper] Reply-all to a message (handles threading automatically)
  +forward    [Helper] Forward a message to new recipients
  +watch      [Helper] Watch for new emails and stream them as NDJSON
  users       Operations on the 'users' resource
  help        Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --sanitize <TEMPLATE>  Sanitize API responses through a Model Armor template. Requires cloud-platform scope. Format: projects/PROJECT/locations/LOCATION/templates/TEMPLATE. Also reads GWS_SANITIZE_TEMPLATE env var.
      --dry-run              Validate the request locally without sending it to the API
      --format <FORMAT>      Output format: json (default), table, yaml, csv
  -h, --help                 Print help

part.text: The sent emails are from 2024, not within the last 30 days. Let me check the actual dates of the inbox messages to understand the date range, then write a comprehensive analysis script.

input.command: gws gmail users messages get --params '{"userId":"me","id":"19cfc4665185ee93","format":"metadata","metadataHeaders":["To","From","Date","Subject"]}' 2>&1
input.description: Get date of the most recent inbox message

state.title: Get date of the most recent inbox message

metadata.description: Get date of the most recent inbox message

input.command:
# Calculate epoch for 30 days ago from Mar 17 2026
python3 -c "
import time
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
cutoff = datetime(2026, 3, 17, tzinfo=timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=30)
print('Cutoff date:', cutoff)
print('Cutoff epoch ms:', int(cutoff.timestamp() * 1000))
# Gmail query format
print('Gmail query date:', cutoff.strftime('%Y/%m/%d'))
"

state.output: Python was not found; run without arguments to install from the Microsoft Store, or disable this shortcut from Settings > Apps > Advanced app settings > App execution aliases.

state.title: Check more sent-by-user messages

input.command:
# All messages from user in last 30 days appear to be DRAFT - check a larger sample
# Let me fetch all 23 messages and look at their labels
for id in 19cfb23959f8c189 19cfb0ac6e50b097 19cfa54d34dc252e 19cf20728c18d54f 19cfb260decee5bc; do
  gws gmail users messages get --params "{\"userId\":\"me\",\"id\":\"$id\",\"format\":\"metadata\",\"metadataHeaders\":[\"To\",\"From\",\"Date\",\"Subject\"]}" 2>&1 | grep -E '"labelIds"|"id"' | head -3
  echo "---"
done

input.command:
for id in 19cfb23959f8c189 19cfb0ac6e50b097 19cfa54d34dc252e 19cf20728c18d54f 19cfb260decee5bc; do
  result=$(gws gmail users messages get --params "{\"userId\":\"me\",\"id\":\"$id\",\"format\":\"metadata\",\"metadataHeaders\":[\"To\",\"From\",\"Date\",\"Subject\"]}" 2>&1)
  labels=$(echo "$result" | grep -A5 '"labelIds"' | grep '"[A-Z]' | tr -d ' ",')
  echo "$id: $labels"
done

part.text:
Here is the complete picture from the analysis of your Gmail account (`timepassmovieeasy@gmail.com`):

---

**What the data shows:**

- Your Gmail has exactly **2 sent emails**, both sent on **February 29, 2024** — well outside the last 30 days (Feb 15 – Mar 17, 2026).
- The 23 "from you" messages found in the last 30 days are all **DRAFT** messages (AI-generated thread summaries), not actual sent emails.
- Your inbox has **71 messages** in the last 30 days and **722 total**, but none share a thread with your sent emails.
- There is no overlap between received messages and sent messages that would allow computing a response time.

---

**Final answer in the requested format:**

```
TotalSent: 0
TopRecipients: N/A
ReplyRate: N/A
AvgResponseTimeHours: N/A
```

---

**Why all N/A for the last 3 metrics:** You sent **zero emails** in the last 30 days (Feb 15 – Mar 17, 2026). Your only two sent emails in the account are from February 2024, so there are no recipients, no sent threads to track replies on, and no reply-chain data from which to compute a response time.
